steps.title: Identify what the passage predicts
steps.content: The decisive clue is that sonic differences “could accumulate to the point of inhibiting interbreeding among fish from different locations.” This forecasts an evolutionary divergence.

steps.title: Match the evolutionary consequence
steps.content: B states exactly what such divergence means: the species “could be in the process of splitting into distinct populations.” A and C mischaracterize the acoustic differences, and D falsely claims they're already genetically distinct. That matches choice B — B is correct.
